title: quercus lanuginosa var. cupaniana
intro: Quercus lanuginosa var. cupaniana, also known as Cupani's Oak, is a variety of deciduous tree native to the Mediterranean region, belonging to the Quercus genus of oaks. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark and leaves, which contain flavonoids and tannins, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Cupani's Oak include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a valuable natural remedy for cardiovascular health and protection against oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of Quercus lanuginosa var. cupaniana include teas, infusions, and extracts, often used to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, fever, and skin conditions.
